title: Otto
content:
{{Top|pfa|leg}}
{{FAreview}}
{{Character
|type=Criminal
|image=[[File:General_Otto.png]]
|name=Otto
|homeworld=[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]<ref name="SWD">''[[Star Wars: Demolition]]''</ref>
|birth=c. [[63 BBY]]<ref name="Age">In the game manual for ''[[Star Wars: Demolition]]'', General Otto was referred to as a 67-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old man. The game itself had both [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] (as [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Tamtel Skreej]]) and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] (as [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]]) in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]], which places its events shortly before {{Film|VI}}, which ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. As such, by the [[Galactic Standard Calendar]], Otto was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[63 BBY]].</ref>
|death=

{{Quote|I am now the ultimate power in the universe!|General Otto|<ref name="Guide">[[Star Wars: Demolition: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Demolition'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>}}
'''Otto''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] who served in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], the armed forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. As a [[General/Legends|general]], he took part in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a conflict between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Despite his status, Otto was regarded as largely incompetent. If it had not been for the intervention of his friend, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Conan Antonio Motti/Legends|Conan Antonio Motti]], Otto would have lost both his career and his life at the hands of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet]]. The general was relocated to the [[Desert/Legends|barren]] planet of [[Lok/Legends|Lok]], where he was tasked with commanding an [[Imperial Outpost (Lok)|Imperial outpost]]. During his tenure on Lok, the general was forced to deal with an intruder who had been sent by the Rebel hero [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] to sow discord in the outpost. Dissatisfied with his situation, General Otto later deserted the Empire and entered [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] [[Jabba's demolition games|demolition games]] with a stolen [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]]. The general hoped he could garner enough [[Currency/Legends|money]] in the contest to fulfill his dream of buying a luxury apartment in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], a floating [[City/Legends|metropolis]] suspended high among the [[Cloud/Legends|clouds]] of the planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]].

==Biography==
===Early life and career===
{{Quote|Long live the Emperor!|General Otto|<ref name="SWD" />}}
A [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="SWG" /> of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]], Otto hailed from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="SWD" /> He<ref name="SWG" /> was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[63 BBY]],<ref name="Age" /> in the final [[Standard year/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> At some point in the [[Imperial Era/Legends|period]] that followed the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by the former [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Otto embraced a career in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]]. A bumbling, insignificant man, the [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] climbed the [[Military rank/Legends|promotion ladder]] only thanks to his connections with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Conan Antonio Motti/Legends|Conan Antonio Motti]],<ref name="CSWEOtto" /> an [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] who had not hesitated to pad his own record himself.<ref name="FF91">[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 91|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 91]]</ref> Eventually, Otto attained the rank of [[Imperial Army/Legends|Army]] [[General/Legends|general]].<ref name="CSWEOtto" />

===Put on the sidelines===
{{Quote|Go disrupt the Imperial base here on Lok. It's commanded by a man named General Otto. I don't think much of him personally. Show him that being on Lok is not an easy assignment.|Han Solo|<ref name="SWG:TIBoL">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=faction|Rebel|The Imperial Base on Lok|Lok}}</ref>}}
[[File:GeneralOttoSWG.jpg|thumb|left|130px|General Otto during his time on Lok]]
As an Imperial general, Otto got involved in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy-wide]] conflict between the Empire and a resistance group called the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Though overzealous and loyal to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] Palpatine to the point of fanaticism, he lost many key battles to the Rebels during the early stages of the war.<ref name="SWG" /> He was also rumored to have been a drug addict.<ref name="SWGTCG">{{SWGTCG|set=Galactic Hunters|cardname=General Otto}}</ref> Otto's career may have been damaged by his major blunders had it not been for another intervention from Admiral Motti, who saved him from the wrath of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the Emperor's second-in-command and [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the Imperial Fleet. Thanks to the machinations of Motti, Otto was quietly shipped to the planet of [[Lok/Legends|Lok]], a desolate world in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], where he was tasked with manning a massive [[Imperial Outpost (Lok)|Imperial outpost]]. On the disused wasteland of Lok, Motti hoped that his embarrassing friend would maintain a low profile.<ref name="SWD" />

Instead of fighting against organized crime on Lok, Otto chose to make an arrangement with its members. The general reached an understanding with [[Nym/Legends|Nym]], the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] leader of the [[Lok Revenants/Legends|Lok Revenants]], promising that his activities would not be interfered with if he refrained from attacking the Imperial outpost.<ref name="SWI65">{{InsiderCite|65|Who's Who in Star Wars Galaxies|Who's Who in ''Star Wars'' Galaxies}}</ref> Despite his arrangements, Otto once faced a brutal assault on his outpost led by a [[Unidentified mercenary warlord|notorious]] local [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], but his troops were able to thwart that attack.<ref name="SWG:MB">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Mozo Bondog|Dathomir}}</ref> The general also experienced trouble with his fellow members of the Empire. According to the discharged [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Moore]], Otto was also at odds with other Imperials on Lok who sought to keep their activities secret from the general. Those activities included a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Imperial Mine (Lok)|facility]] where gases and [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]] were extracted,<ref name="SWG:SwSM">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Speak with Sergeant Moore|Lok}}</ref> and the associated [[Imperial Research Facility (Lok)|research facility]], where a [[Imperial Military Grade Grenade Casing Unit|new type]] of [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]] was being developed.<ref name="SWG:SwSMa">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Speak with Sergeant Moore, again|Lok}}</ref>

At some point following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], a defunct [[R4 astromech droid/Legends|R4-series]] [[agromech droid]] that had once belonged to Otto fell into the clutches of the [[Sulfur Lake Pirates]], who maintained a [[Sulfur Lake Pirate Hideout|hideout]] on Lok. The [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] [[Memory bank/Legends|memory core]], however, ended up in the hands of the Lok Revenant [[Vana Sage]], who wanted to blackmail Otto based on its contents.<ref name="SWG:RtDD">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Retrieve the Droid Disk|Lok}}</ref> Around the same time, the Rebel hero [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] sent one of his agents to cause mayhem in the Imperial outpost and show General Otto that being assigned on a backwater planet was not going to be easy. Otto confronted the Rebel agent that had come to torment him,<ref name="SWG:TIBoL" /> but despite being a [[Martial artist/Legends|martial artist]],<ref name="SWGGuide">[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Total Experience: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: The Total Experience'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> the Imperial was beaten during the showdown. However, the general successfully escaped his opponent before being killed. Ultimately, the Rebel intruder returned to Solo to claim their reward, leaving Otto with combat injuries and hurt feelings.<ref name="SWG:TIBoL" />

===Entering Jabba's games===
[[File:Ottos_walker.png|thumb|right|200px|The Imperial AT-ST, Otto's weapon of choice]]
{{Quote|I'm booking passage to Bespin!|Otto|<ref name="SWD" />}}
Such bad encounters and the sad spectacle of Lok's [[Desert/Legends|barren]] landscape made Otto dwell on his dream of retiring to a luxury apartment on [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], a haven of airy platforms, spires, and towers floating in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. Eventually, in pursuit of that dream, Otto stole a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] and abandoned the Empire, taking an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] bipedal [[Walker/Legends|walker]] with him. Wanted by Imperial forces for desertion, Otto traveled to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]],<ref name="SWD" /> a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]-controlled planet of the Outer Rim.<ref name="CL">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref> Around [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="SWDdate">The game ''[[Star Wars: Demolition]]'' featured [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] infiltrating [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] as [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Tamtel Skreej]] and [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]], which was shown in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. According to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', the events of ''Episode VI'' occurred in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> he had entered the [[Jabba's demolition games|demolition contests]] organized by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], hoping to gather the riches he needed to achieve his dream. On the contest field of the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]], Otto confronted various worthy adversaries such as the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]], the Human [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named [[Wade Vox]], the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Pugwis]] and the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] engineer [[Quagga]]. The general also faced [[Malakili/Legends|Malakili]], the keeper of Jabba's [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]], as well as other accomplices of the Hutt, such as [[Wittin/Legends|Wittin]] the [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]]. Unbeknownst to the general, three heroes of the Rebel Alliance had also entered the derby: the [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] and Rebel leader [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], disguised as the bounty hunter [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]], the former [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of Cloud City [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], posing as a guard named [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Tamtel Skreej]], and the latter's [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] assistant [[Lobot/Legends|Lobot]].<ref name="SWD" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I will not be stopped! Mmuahahaha!|Otto|<ref name="SWD" />}}
[[File:Otto.png|thumb|left|Otto at the time of the demolition games]]
While he was manning the Lok outpost, General Otto sported dark [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and a horseshoe-shaped mustache.<ref name="SWG" /> However, by the time of Jabba's demolition derby, the general looked very different. He had gained excessive weight, his hair had gone entirely white, and he had grown a full beard with two points.<ref name="SWD" /> As an officer, Otto was thought to lack dignity in his manners and appearance,<ref name="SWGTCG" /> and even the Rebel hero Han Solo thought of him as unimportant, rather than a serious threat.<ref name="SWG:TIBoL" /> At the time, Otto had no compunction about turning a blind eye to Lok's criminality, and the climate between the general and the other Imperials stationed there remained unsettled.<ref name="SWG" />

In spite of his grandfatherly looks, his fancy [[uniform]]<ref name="SWD" /> and his poor reputation,<ref name="SWG" /> Otto was still a dangerous individual. As a derby fighter, he suffered delusions of grandeur and a misplaced sense of superiority that fueled his sense of pugnacity.<ref name="SWD" /> He was convinced that he had become the most powerful being in the [[Universe/Legends|Known Universe]]<ref name="Guide" /> and that nothing or no one could stop him.<ref name="SWD" /> Among his many flaws, if rumors about him were true, the general was addicted to the mind-altering [[Spice/Legends|spice]].<ref name="SWGTCG" /> In fact, Otto would sometimes threaten his defeated opponents with a transfer to the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|Spice Mines]] of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. Despite being a deserter and a self-obsessed man, General Otto maintained a certain degree of loyalty toward the Empire and Palpatine, as he would sometimes enter the demolition contests yelling "Long live the Emperor!"<ref name="SWD" />

==Skills and abilities==
{{Quote|You're headed to the spice mines, scum!|Otto|<ref name="SWD" />}}
As part of his Imperial officer training, General Otto was a master of ranged combat using [[Blaster carbine/Legends|blaster carbines]]. He had also learned the rudiments of [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|Teräs Käsi]], a [[Unarmed combat/Legends|weaponless]] martial art.<ref name="SWGGuide" /> He was also capable of piloting his modified AT-ST all by himself,<ref name="SWD" /> while manning that vehicle normally required two crewmen, a [[Vehicle Command Crewer|pilot]], and a [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]].<ref name="BtM">''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref>

==Equipment==
In the Lok Imperial Outpost, Otto simply wore the Empire's [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|standard uniform]], which consisted of a double-breasted grey tunic with matching trousers, black [[Leather/Legends|leather]] [[Belt/Legends|belt]], black [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and a [[Hat/Legends|hat]]. On the left side of the upper chest, he sported a single [[Imperial code cylinder]] and the [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|insignia]] of an army general. His cap and belt were also equipped with an [[Officer's disk/Legends|officer's disc]],<ref name="SWG" /> which contained coded information.<ref name="BtM" /> When taking part in Jabba's contests, he wore a garish version of an [[Imperial Army trooper/Legends|Imperial Army trooper]] dress uniform with red pants, a [[Gold/Legends|golden]] plastron, a blue cape and a wide array of medals and festoons. He also wore a fancy rank plaque made of twelve golden squares.<ref name="SWD" />

==Behind the scenes==
General Otto first appeared as a playable character in the [[2000]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Demolition]]'', a vehicular combat [[Star Wars video games|video game]] featuring characters, locations and vehicles from around the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe. Voice actor [[Tom Kane]] voiced Otto in that game, along with [[Bib Fortuna/Legends|Bib Fortuna]], Boba Fett and Lobot.<ref name="SWD" />

In ''Star Wars: Demolition'', depending on the player's success, Otto could either win the tournament, which led him to fulfill his dream, or lose it, which had him turned over to a pair of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] by one of his fellow contestants, Boba Fett, and ultimately left at Darth Vader's mercy.<ref name="SWD" /> Otto's AT-ST walker was the first vehicle created for the game. It was also a favorite of the graphician [[Edvard Toth]], who modeled some and textured all vehicles in ''Star Wars: Demolition''.<ref name="Toth">{{WebCite|url=http://edvardtoth.com/games/star-wars-demolition/#.UfbKP417JXN|text=Star Wars: Demolition|work=Edvard Toth's website|archivedate=20171206011520}}</ref>

[[File:ImperialCorporalTemplate.jpg|250px|thumb|All possible depictions of General Otto in ''Star Wars Galaxies'' prior to the 2007 update]]
The general also appeared in the now-defunct [[2003]] [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''.<ref name="SWG" /> Originally, his appearance randomized under one of six possible generic Imperial officer depictions (without [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|rank insignia plaque]]) and would fluctuate after each server reset.<ref name="SWG" /> Otto in ''Star Wars Galaxies'' did not appear like he did in ''Star Wars: Demolition''.<ref name="SWG" /><ref name="SWD" /> However, the events depicted in ''Galaxies'' occurred in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> a few years at best before Jabba's demolition games.<ref name="SWD" /> With the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 6 Notes for May 22 2007|Chapter 6]]" of ''Star Wars Galaxies'', on [[May 22]], [[2007]],<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Notes – Chapter 6: Masters of the Wild">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=67250&month=052007|text=Publish Notes – Chapter 6: Masters of the Wild}}</ref> Otto's appearance was changed by the developers to a unique appearance so he would wear a mustache, but he still looked younger, taller, and thinner than his ''Demolition'' incarnation.<ref name="SWG:TIBoL" />

With the release of the "Chapter 6" of ''Galaxies'', General Otto was integrated into the revamped "[[W:c:swg:Rebel Theme Park|Rebel Theme Park]]." During the quest "The Imperial Base on Lok," Rebel players were tasked by Han Solo with fighting and killing Otto. In actuality, the general would not die. Once the player got his health to 10%, Otto would yell "Aaaah! Get away from me!!" and run away by despawning.<ref name="SWG:TIBoL" />
